must_not_assert: remote access was established or the host was breached (the inbound-allow rule is added but no inbound connection uses it and no follow-on activity confirms malice)

the_trap: An inbound-allow firewall rule is added via netsh and takes effect, but the same rule is deleted about five seconds later. No inbound connection uses it and no follow-on activity confirms malice. The cleanup is mitigating context, but it does not alone prove benign intent; do not assert that remote access was established or the host breached.
rationale: A0 runs 'netsh advfirewall firewall add rule action=allow dir=in'; EV-001 is the FirewallRules registry entry (Action=Allow, Active=TRUE) and EV-002 the SharedAccess firewall Epoch bump confirming that the rule took effect. EV-003 deletes the same named TCP/450 rule about five seconds later and EV-004 records the matching registry deletion. Opening an inbound port is security-relevant, but the short lifetime, absent use, and absent follow-on leave intent unconfirmed: suspicious, investigate.
